======= RMM01936Emily Boardman estate papers1936Compiled by A. GatesDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Emily Boardman estate papers
Boardman, Emily, -1898.
1936
82 items. 82 items.
1888-1903. A will, accounts, receipts, and correspondence. Will, accounts, receipts, and correspondence of Emily Boardman, addressed to George R. Williams pertaining to the settlement of her estate. Collection material in English 2078623
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
A. Gates
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2004
Date modified:
Jude Corina, October 2013
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Emily Boardman, of Trumansburg, New York, was a sister of Douglass Boardman, an Ithaca, New York lawyer.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Will, accounts, receipts, and correspondence of Emily Boardman, addressed to George R. Williams pertaining to the settlement of her estate.
Cite As:
Emily Boardman estate papers, #1936. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Wills. Accounts. Williams, George R.
2078623 <<<<<<< HEAD Will of Emily Boardman of Trumansburg, New York. Heirs named in will are: sisters Maria Sears, Delia Hathaway, Patty Spence, Gertrude Markham, Lucy B. Smith; brothers Russell Boardman, Harry Boardman, Truman Boardman, Douglass Boardman; nephew George H. Smith; and niece Ellen D. Williams.Nov. 17, 188811 - Folder 1Copy of contract for payment of the Lewis Investment Co. bonds by Emily Boardman to Cornell University.Dec. 5, 189611 - Folder 1Warranty Deed between the heirs of J.B. Williams and Charles Thomas of Ithaca, N.Y. seeling to Thomas land located in the town of Ithaca. The heirs are Mrs. May H. Williams, George R. Williams and Ellen B. his wife, Roger B. Williams and Caroline R. his wife, Henry S. Williams and Harriet H. his wife, Augusta H. Williams, Charlotte E. Williams, Mrs. Jane W. Newman and Jared T. her husband, Ella S. Williams, Clara M. Williams, Otis L. Williams, all of Ithaca, New York.Feb. 10, 189811 - Folder 1Account of the Emily Boardman estate.Apr. 25, 189811 - Folder 1Three (3) legal documents stating that George R. Williams of Ithaca, New York is the executor of the Emily Boardman estate.July 11, 189811 - Folder 1Investments showing change of the Emily Boardman estate since the last statement.Sept. 22, 189811 - Folder 1Tompkins Co., N.Y. Copy of the Appraiser's report on the estate of Emily Boardman.Oct. 8, 189811 - Folder 1Legal document accepting the appraiser's report on the estate of Emily Boardman.Oct. 22, 189811 - Folder 1Letter from Miss Frances Boardman of Los Angeles, Calif. to George R. Williams concerning the death of her father and the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Feb. 8, 1900 (1901?)11 - Folder 1Fifty-eight (58) receipts to George R. Williams, as executor of the Emily Boardman estate, for dividend No. 2 on her estate. Signed by members of the Boardman, Sears, Spence, and Hathaway families, and numerous others.Feb. 1, 190111 - Folder 1Letter from Miss Frances Boardman to George R. Williams concerning the payment of a draft Williams sent her.Feb. 27, 190111 - Folder 1Letter from Theodore Spence of Starkey, N.Y. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Riley Spence estate.Apr. (?) 23, 190211 - Folder 1Letter from Mrs. Emma G. Myrick of Palmyra, Iowa to George R. Williams concerning the payment to her of her share of the Emily Boardman estate.Nov. 5, 190211 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the death of Maria S. Blakeslee and settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Feb. 24, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Mrs. C. Blakeslee of St. Augustine, Florida to George R. Williams concerning Frances Boardman, an heir of Emily Boardman.Mar. 8, 1903 (?)11 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.July 28, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Nov. 5, 190311 - Folder 1Statement of account of estate of Emily Boardman.Dec. 1, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Maria S. Blakeslee and Emily Boardman estates.Dec. 4, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Grace H. Thian of Minneapolis, Minnesota to George R. Williams giving him the addresses of two relatives of Emily Boardman.Dec. 11, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Theodore Spence of Dundee, N.Y. to George R. Williams giving him the addresses of several members of the Spence family.Dec. 14, 190311 - Folder 1 ======= Will of Emily Boardman of Trumansburg, New York. Heirs named in will are: sisters Maria Sears, Delia Hathaway, Patty Spence, Gertrude Markham, Lucy B. Smith; brothers Russell Boardman, Harry Boardman, Truman Boardman, Douglass Boardman; nephew George H. Smith; and niece Ellen D. Williams.Nov. 17, 188811 - Folder 1Copy of contract for payment of the Lewis Investment Co. bonds by Emily Boardman to Cornell University.Dec. 5, 189611 - Folder 1Warranty Deed between the heirs of J.B. Williams and Charles Thomas of Ithaca, N.Y. seeling to Thomas land located in the town of Ithaca. The heirs are Mrs. May H. Williams, George R. Williams and Ellen B. his wife, Roger B. Williams and Caroline R. his wife, Henry S. Williams and Harriet H. his wife, Augusta H. Williams, Charlotte E. Williams, Mrs. Jane W. Newman and Jared T. her husband, Ella S. Williams, Clara M. Williams, Otis L. Williams, all of Ithaca, New York.Feb. 10, 189811 - Folder 1Account of the Emily Boardman estate.Apr. 25, 189811 - Folder 1Three (3) legal documents stating that George R. Williams of Ithaca, New York is the executor of the Emily Boardman estate.July 11, 189811 - Folder 1Investments showing change of the Emily Boardman estate since the last statement.Sept. 22, 189811 - Folder 1Tompkins Co., N.Y. Copy of the Appraiser's report on the estate of Emily Boardman.Oct. 8, 189811 - Folder 1Legal document accepting the appraiser's report on the estate of Emily Boardman.Oct. 22, 189811 - Folder 1Letter from Miss Frances Boardman of Los Angeles, Calif. to George R. Williams concerning the death of her father and the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Feb. 8, 1900 (1901?)11 - Folder 1Fifty-eight (58) receipts to George R. Williams, as executor of the Emily Boardman estate, for dividend No. 2 on her estate. Signed by members of the Boardman, Sears, Spence, and Hathaway families, and numerous others.Feb. 1, 190111 - Folder 1Letter from Miss Frances Boardman to George R. Williams concerning the payment of a draft Williams sent her.Feb. 27, 190111 - Folder 1Letter from Theodore Spence of Starkey, N.Y. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Riley Spence estate.Apr. (?) 23, 190211 - Folder 1Letter from Mrs. Emma G. Myrick of Palmyra, Iowa to George R. Williams concerning the payment to her of her share of the Emily Boardman estate.Nov. 5, 190211 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the death of Maria S. Blakeslee and settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Feb. 24, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Mrs. C. Blakeslee of St. Augustine, Florida to George R. Williams concerning Frances Boardman, an heir of Emily Boardman.Mar. 8, 1903 (?)11 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.July 28, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Emily Boardman estate.Nov. 5, 190311 - Folder 1Statement of account of estate of Emily Boardman.Dec. 1, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from C.M. Masters of Sparta, Wis. to George R. Williams concerning the settlement of the Maria S. Blakeslee and Emily Boardman estates.Dec. 4, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Grace H. Thian of Minneapolis, Minnesota to George R. Williams giving him the addresses of two relatives of Emily Boardman.Dec. 11, 190311 - Folder 1Letter from Theodore Spence of Dundee, N.Y. to George R. Williams giving him the addresses of several members of the Spence family.Dec. 14, 190311 - Folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
